top5.title: AI Investment Bubble Threat to Economy
top5.reach: 57% of economists view tech valuation collapse as greatest 2026 risk; major tech companies investing $500B+ annually while consumer spending remains under $12B yearly.
top5.the_fear: Massive AI infrastructure investment could create an unsustainable economic bubble that bursts and triggers recession or financial crisis.
top5.why_contested: Supporters note AI companies like Nvidia generate real profits and revenue unlike dot-com bubble; critics point to 95% of firms reporting zero ROI on GenAI despite massive spending and heavy debt financing.
top5.case_for: Investment vastly exceeds consumer revenue, financial engineering obscures systemic risks, and concentration matches dot-com peak valuations at 35% of S&P 500.
top5.case_against: Fed Chair Powell distinguishes AI from dot-com era citing real corporate revenues; sovereign AI demand globally provides cushion; established tech has diversified business models beyond AI.
questions: Will massive capex translate to profitable returns or massive write-downs?
Can precarious private credit markets absorb AI-related defaults?
Is this transformative infrastructure or temporary overinvestment?

top5.title: Social Media Harm to Children Level
top5.reach: Multiple countries from Australia to Norway to US debating age restrictions; jury found Meta and YouTube liable March 2026 in landmark case; global scientific consensus disputes harm severity.
top5.the_fear: Unregulated social media platforms are harming youth mental health at population scale through addictive design and psychological damage.
top5.why_contested: Experts disagree whether causation is proven versus correlation; some cite evidence of harm, others argue benefits coexist with harms and bans won't address underlying drivers.
top5.case_for: Hospital admissions for self-harm in girls ages 10-14 doubled 2010-2014; major depression increases correlate with social media adoption; platforms use acknowledged addictive design features.
top5.case_against: American Psychological Association finds social media can be both beneficial and harmful; 25,000-adolescent longitudinal study found no relationship between time spent and distress; drivers predate internet.
questions: Does restricting access address harm or just hide it on darker platforms?
Are causation concerns overblown relative to child welfare precaution?
Can regulation of design features work better than age bans?

top5.title: Iran Military Threat and War Risk
top5.reach: Trump threatened to bomb Iran's civilian infrastructure in early April; ceasefire announced April 7 but remains fragile with contradictory signals from all sides.
top5.the_fear: Escalating US-Iran military conflict could expand to catastrophic regional war destabilizing global oil markets and creating humanitarian crisis.
top5.why_contested: Trump administration claims military pressure forced Iranian concessions; critics argue threats target civilian infrastructure, violate war crimes law, and destabilize negotiations; Iran remains defiant.
top5.case_for: Iran is advancing nuclear program, threatening regional allies, and closing Strait of Hormuz; military pressure extracted ceasefire and reopened critical shipping route.
top5.case_against: Threats to destroy civilian power plants and water facilities constitute war crimes; ongoing blockade and threats undermine credibility of negotiations; ceasefire repeatedly near collapse.
questions: Will ceasefire hold or collapse under renewed threats and Israeli operations in Lebanon?
Do military threats reduce or increase long-term Iranian nuclear ambitions?
What international consequences follow US targeting of civilian infrastructure?

top5.title: Gun Violence Threat Level Consensus
top5.reach: Data shows Q1 2026 had lowest shooting deaths in 12 years (3,103 deaths); 48 states down significantly since 2021-2023; yet Virginia passed 25 gun reforms and federal bill proposed.
top5.the_fear: Uncontrolled gun violence continues killing thousands annually and mass shootings remain endemic national security threat.
top5.why_contested: Gun deaths declining but remain politically contested; supporters cite decreases as vindication of current policy, opponents call continued deaths unacceptable and demand stricter measures.
top5.case_for: 3,100+ deaths annually despite declines; Virginia shooting data shows 132 incidents in 4 months; jury found social media companies liable as partial cause of youth harm.
top5.case_against: Largest decline in a dozen years suggests existing policies and enforcement working; most deaths are suicides not mass shootings; Second Amendment constitutional protection limits regulation.
questions: Do declining numbers suggest problem solved or still unacceptable baseline?
Can gun regulations pass courts or violate Second Amendment interpretations?
What drives dramatic 2021-2023 spike and subsequent drop if not policy changes?

top5.title: China AI and Intellectual Property Threat
top5.reach: Trump administration accused China April 24 of stealing AI lab intellectual property on industrial scale; yet summit planned next month amid competition framing.
top5.the_fear: China's systematic theft of US artificial intelligence research could allow rapid advancement enabling military and economic dominance.
top5.why_contested: Officials characterize theft as existential threat; Beijing views reverse-engineering and parallel development as normal competition; experts debate whether espionage or market dynamics matter more.
top5.case_for: Official accusations of industrial-scale IP theft; cyber agencies warn of sophisticated covert networks; national security requires protection of AI technology.
top5.case_against: DeepSeek's success showed China developing capable AI independently; US companies operate within China and collect similar data; theft claims often conflate legitimate and illegal activity.
questions: How does Chinese AI development compare to US in actual capability versus political narrative?
Can IP protection balance innovation openness with security?
Is AI dominance determined by stolen research or fundamental investment and talent?

honorable_mentions.title: White House Correspondents Dinner Security Incident
honorable_mentions.summary: Shooting at Washington Hilton during Trump event; suspect arrested; triggered debate over security measures and Trump's planned underground White House ballroom.

honorable_mentions.title: Iran Negotiations Collapse and Revival
honorable_mentions.summary: Trump canceled Pakistan peace delegation visit citing Iran infighting but talks resuming; multiple deadline extensions and backtracking fuel uncertainty over settlement durability.

honorable_mentions.title: NATO Unity Against Russia Concerns
honorable_mentions.summary: Trump administration threatening NATO withdrawal; European allies alarmed; Netherlands intelligence warns greatest security threat since WWII from Russia and China.

honorable_mentions.title: US-Argentina Falkland Islands Sovereignty Dispute
honorable_mentions.summary: Trump administration signaling willingness to reconsider US recognition of British control; Argentina has long disputed sovereignty; first official US position shift in decades.

honorable_mentions.title: AI Bias and Military Ethics Controversy
honorable_mentions.summary: Defense Secretary Hegseth characterized Anthropic's safety guidelines as corporate virtue-signaling; Pentagon blacklisting major US AI firm creates rift between safety-first labs and national security demands.
